segments.text: When the morning was come, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death
And when they had bound him, they led him away, and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor
Then Judas, which had betrayed him, when he saw that he was condemned, repented himself, and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders
I have sinned in that I have betrayed the innocent blood
segments.speaker: Judas

segments.text: What is that to us? see thou to that
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Elders

segments.text: And Jesus stood before the governor
Art thou the King of the Jews?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Whom will ye that I release unto you? Barabbas, or Jesus which is called Christ?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: I am innocent of the blood of this just person: see ye to it
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly this was the Son of God
segments.speaker: Centurion
